title: Canonical Policy Registry
commands_run: rg drift scans for old FAE rule meanings, stale repo-root policy paths, and prohibited attribution-style source lines
py -m json.tool on all changed JSON files, including the Phase 02 report JSON
PowerShell ConvertFrom-Json on all changed JSON files, including the Phase 02 report JSON
PowerShell canonical ID check for exactly FAE-C001 through FAE-C012
PowerShell SHA-256 mirror check for core/root compliance, repo-boundary, and docs-sync registries
Python Markdown alignment check comparing registry titles against COMPLIANCE_POLICY.md and wiki/Compliance.md
Visual Studio Developer Command environment followed by powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/validate-repo.ps1
mcp_and_tooling_used: local filesystem operations through the shared Windows workspace
local code execution with py and PowerShell
Visual Studio Developer Command environment for repository validation
VS Code CLI discovery confirmed local IDE availability

local_first_and_provider_fallback_decisi.notes: All implementation and validation used local repository files and local machine tooling.

subagents.finding: Phase 02 needed a canonical core registry, byte-identical root mirror, breaking-change classification, migration guidance, and expanded documentation scope.
Markdown/JSON rule drift and stale repo-boundary paths needed correction; later contributor-guard behavior stayed out of Phase 02.

Documentation Manager blocked for protected-path/documentation drift; Validator blocked for pending reviewer evidence and incomplete report fields; Architect requested changelog breaking-field changes; Security requested docs-sync canonical path and executor/authorizer clarifications. All findings were remediated before final recheck.
passed for architect-reviewer, security-auditor, Documentation Manager, and Validator; no remaining remediation required
